lever.name: QR-preprocess tall full-rank pinv
lever.description: Route tall full-rank m>=2n real pseudoinverse through thin QR, compute pinv(R) by SVD, then form R_plus * Q_transpose.

rch_baseline_first.note: This is the existing public pinv bench and was run before edits/proof work. The later full-rank A/B is the primary gate because the existing data is low rank and routes through the already-landed low-rank path.

behavior_proof.candidate_behavior: The candidate was not bit-isomorphic to direct SVD because QR preprocessing changes floating-point operation order. The intended proof obligation was Moore-Penrose uniqueness plus singular-value cutoff equivalence with deterministic tolerance goldens.
behavior_proof.accepted_state: Rejected state restores production and benchmark source exactly to parent 9ab4f5ea; therefore public behavior, ordering, tie-breaking, floating-point recurrence, RNG absence, dtype/shape/error surface, and low-rank dispatch remain unchanged.

proof_gates.source_restored: git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs crates/fj-lax/benches/lax_baseline.rs produced no output before closeout commit
proof_gates.rch_tests: ovh-a: cargo test -p fj-lax --lib pinv -- --nocapture; 8 passed, 0 failed
proof_gates.rch_clippy: ovh-a: cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings; passed
proof_gates.ubs: ubs crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs crates/fj-lax/benches/lax_baseline.rs exited 1 on pre-existing broad inventory; no unsafe blocks, and UBS fmt/clippy/check/test-build sections were clean

next_route.do_not_repeat: Do not retry QR-preprocess-at-2n for pinv256 full-rank; QR plus R_plus*Q_transpose is slower than direct one-sided Jacobi SVD on the same worker.
next_route.deeper_primitive: Attack the SVD core itself: blocked/one-sided Jacobi sweeps with cache-resident column tiles, fewer full-column passes, or a Golub-Kahan bidiagonalization artifact with an explicit tolerance contract.
